icp_reason: On-prem AD environment with greenfield SIEM gap, post-breach remediation mandate, CJIS compliance pressure, and confirmed Microsoft stack maps precisely to the AD360 + Log360 target profile for sub-500 FTE public-sector organizations.
executive_brief: City of Coppell (291-FTE municipality, Dallas County TX) runs on-premises AD + One Identity Active Roles with zero SIEM coverage. RansomHub stole 442 GB and exposed 17,000 residents' SSNs in Oct 2024; investigation closed June 2025. $159.1M FY2026 budget with public safety IT as top priority, CJIS mandate active, Texas Cyber Command enforcement launching 2026. AD360 + Log360 fill the exact gap the breach exposed. Gabriel Colon-Atencio (gabriel.colon@coppelltx.gov, Asst Director ES) is the inbound technical evaluator; champion Josh Littrell (Director ES, Dec 2024) owns the cybersecurity roadmap.
verdict_next: Step 1 (Week 1): Email gabriel.colon@coppelltx.gov with a technically specific, sub-150-word opener referencing the October 2024 breach and the specific gap between One Identity Active Roles (provisioning only) and log correlation/AD threat detection. Co…
demo_playbook: {"persona": "Gabriel Colon-Atencio \u2014 Assistant Director of Enterprise Solutions, CompTIA Security+ certified, former DynCorp Army airfield network ops; evaluates tools as an operator, not a buyer; co-managed the October 2024 RansomHub breach recovery and will run the technical evaluation for any new security tooling.", "opening_hook": "Gabriel, your October 2024 incident report and the AG catastrophe notice tell a specific story: One Identity Active Roles did exactly what it was designed to do \u2014 it provisioned the accounts correctly. What it couldn't do is watch what those accounts did after login. That's not a gap in One Identity \u2014 it's a gap in your detection layer, and it's why RansomHub had enough time to move 442 GB before your automated systems flagged it. We built AD360 and Log360 specifically for on-prem AD environments like yours \u2014 no Entra ID migration required, no cloud dependency. I'd like to show you the two screens your team would have seen that morning if these tools had been in place. 15 minutes \u2014 can we do Thursday at 2pm?", "has_ad360": true, "has_log360": true, "has_playbook": true}
verdict_insight: Reference the October 2024 RansomHub breach specifically and the gap between One Identity Active Roles (provisioning) and detection/correlation; offer DIR/TXMAS cooperative purchasing availability as friction-reducer

icp_reason: Government vertical and greenfield SIEM posture are strong fits; headcount (145) is below ICP sweet spot and council-manager governance constrains deal authority.
executive_brief: WARM lead — City of Bellaire (145 FTEs, Harris County TX enclave municipality). Jorge Aguirre is Director of IT, 3.5-yr tenure (approaching peak 12-18mo decision window passed; mid-cycle). CJIS + Texas DIR/TAC 202 compliance pressure; Police Dept triggers mandatory CJIS audit-log requirements; no confirmed SIEM or IAM tooling detected — greenfield opportunity. Deal size ~$29K (bundle). Pursue with compliance-gap angle.
verdict_next: Within 5 business days: Send Slot 1 Compliance Gap email — cite CJIS v6.0 Section 5.4 audit-logging mandate; reference Texas DIR/TAC 202 dual pressure; offer one-page CJIS-to-Log360 control mapping as value-give
demo_playbook: {"persona": "IT Director at a 145-FTE Texas enclave municipality with Bellaire PD as a CJIS-bound unit. Jorge has CCNP + MCSE credentials and pharma compliance background \u2014 technically fluent, ROI-oriented, no-nonsense.", "opening_hook": "Before I show you anything \u2014 let me ask: if your CJIS auditor called tomorrow and asked for the access audit trail for your PD's domain controllers for the last 90 days, how long would it take you to pull that? Most IT directors I talk to say 'a few hours' or 'I'd need to manually pull Event Viewer from each box.' Log360 makes that a 30-second report. That's the opening.", "has_ad360": true, "has_log360": true, "has_playbook": true}
verdict_insight: CJIS v6.0 Section 5.4 audit-log retention gap — one-page CJIS-to-Log360 control mapping as value-give

icp_reason: Small on-prem Windows AD environment, no incumbent tooling, regulated PHI/PII data, multiple concurrent compliance deadlines — textbook AD360/Log360 ICP for a nonprofit fund office
executive_brief: Chicago Laborers Funds (CHILPWF) is a 30-person ERISA/HIPAA-covered multi-employer trust in Westchester IL holding PHI+SSN data on thousands of members. No SIEM, no MFA, no DMARC detected — greenfield IAM+log opportunity. DOL CAR 2024-01 (Sept 2024) + HIPAA 2026 final rule + Illinois PIPA create concurrent fiduciary liability NOW. Lead: Rini Torano, Director IS, 13yr tenure.
verdict_next: Step 1: Within 3 business days — Send a single email to rinit@chilpwf.com (<150 words) referencing DOL EBSA CAR 2024-01 and HIPAA 2026 by name as specific regulatory requirements applicable to her fund; reference a comparable Taft-Hartley fund use case; …
company: Chicago & Vicinity Laborers' District Council Pension & Welfare Funds
demo_playbook: {"persona": "Rini Torano, Director of Information Services \u2014 sole IT leader at a 30-person multi-employer benefits fund administering pension and health/welfare benefits for construction laborers; responsible for a legacy on-prem Windows environment, a new ESS employer portal, and compliance with DOL EBSA + HIPAA + PIPA simultaneously, with a small IT team and Board-approval governance overhead", "opening_hook": "Rini, I work with a handful of Taft-Hartley fund offices similar to yours \u2014 pension and health-and-welfare plans with on-prem Windows environments and small IT teams managing a lot of regulatory exposure. The reason I reached out specifically is that the DOL's September 2024 CAR guidance and the HIPAA Security Rule update that went final earlier this year both include requirements \u2014 mandatory MFA, access audit logs, annual recertification \u2014 that are genuinely hard to evidence with native Windows tools alone. I wanted to spend 20 minutes understanding how you're currently set up, and share what a couple of comparable fund offices are doing to close those gaps without adding headcount.", "has_ad360": true, "has_log360": true, "has_playbook": true}
verdict_insight: DOL EBSA CAR 2024-01 + HIPAA 2026 final rule convergence creates fiduciary liability for CHILPWF trustees — referencing a comparable Taft-Hartley fund use case opens the compliance-first conversation without a product pitch
